content:
1,636.

That is the number of documented cases where AI produced hallucinated content in legal proceedings.

Updated yesterday. 👀

Not 729 - the number we referenced two weeks ago.

1,636. And growing.

A researcher has been tracking every case where a court or tribunal found, or implied, that a party relied on hallucinated AI content. Not allegations. Confirmed cases. On the record.

In the time it has taken most law firms to schedule an AI policy meeting, this number has more than doubled.

And in 6 days, the Colorado AI Act takes effect.

Requiring risk management policies, impact assessments, and transparency for high-risk AI systems - in a category that explicitly includes legal services.

So here is where we are, today, on this Tuesday:

1,636 confirmed AI hallucination cases in legal proceedings.
6 days until the first comprehensive AI law covering legal services takes effect.
78% of the legal market still without an AI strategy.
And a profession still largely untrained on the tools it is already using. ⚖️

This is not a slow-moving crisis.

It is moving faster than the profession is responding.

The lawyers who will be on the right side of this are not waiting for their firm, their regulator, or their law school to tell them what to do.

They are learning now.

That is the only thing The AI Bar has ever asked of anyone.

📌 8 courses. Built by practising lawyers.
👉 No tech background required.
Dropping soon.

Follow the page. Drop your email in our inbox/comments for early access and the full course breakdown.
#LegalTech #AIForLawyers #TheAIBar #AIHallucination #LegalCompliance
